title: keep-id and ping
body:
### Issue Description

It seems that `--userns=keep-id` and ping interact in a somewhat weird way and ping isn't allowed for the group that is identical to the user group (see example below).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue (this assumes that your rootless user and group ID is 1000:1000)
1. Add this to `/etc/containers/containers.conf`:
   ```text
   default_sysctls = [
       "net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 65536",
   ]
   ```
3. `podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:1000 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8`


### Describe the results you received

```text
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
ping: permission denied (are you root?)
```

### Describe the results you expected

Ping is working.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

# User and Group IDs

```console
$ id -u
1000
$ id -g
1000
```

### Additional information

# Test w/ `keep-id`
This is the scenario described in this bug that doesn't work:

```console
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:1000 alpine id
uid=1000(mneumann) gid=1000(mneumann) groups=1000(mneumann)
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:1000 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
ping: permission denied (are you root?)
```

# Test w/o `keep-id`
If we don't use `keep-id`, it works:

```console
$ podman run -it --rm -u1000:1000 alpine id
uid=1000(mneumann) gid=1000(1000) groups=1000(1000)
$ podman run -it --rm -u1000:1000 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=0 ttl=42 time=17.119 ms

--- 8.8.8.8 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 17.119/17.119/17.119 ms
```

# Test w/ different user/group
If we use a different user/group, it also works:

```console
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u999:999 alpine id
uid=999(999) gid=999(ping) groups=1000(mneumann),999(ping)
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u999:999 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=0 ttl=42 time=14.373 ms

--- 8.8.8.8 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 14.373/14.373/14.373 ms
```

```console
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1001:1001 alpine id
uid=1001(1001) gid=1001(1001) groups=1000(mneumann),1001(1001)
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1001:1001 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=0 ttl=42 time=17.239 ms

--- 8.8.8.8 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 17.239/17.239/17.239 ms
```

This can also be reproduced when you only change the group:

```console
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:999 alpine id
uid=1000(mneumann) gid=999(ping) groups=1000(mneumann),999(ping)
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:999 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=0 ttl=42 time=14.890 ms

--- 8.8.8.8 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 14.890/14.890/14.890 ms
```

```console
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:1001 alpine id
uid=1000(mneumann) gid=1001(1001) groups=1000(mneumann),1001(1001)
$ podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id -u1000:1001 alpine ping -c1 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=0 ttl=42 time=19.639 ms

--- 8.8.8.8 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 19.639/19.639/19.639 ms
```

comments.body:
this smells like the kernel is converting the specified limits to `ping_group_range` from the user namespace to the kernel gids.

```
default_sysctls = [
    "net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 65536",
]
```

In the example above, 0 is in reality a high ID with keep-id (since it is mapped to the first additional ID), so they are converted like: 0 -> 100000 and 65536 -> 165535.

So when you try to create an ICMP socket with your gid=1000, the kernel will reject it since 1000 is not included in 100000-165535.

To get the expected behavior, you'll need to specify something like: `--sysctl='net.ipv4.ping_group_range=1000 1000'`.

I am closing the issue because this is not something Podman can fix (except getting into the semantic of the specific sysctl), and it is a kernel behavior but feel free to comment further
